summary: Integration reverses differentiation. The indefinite integral (antiderivative) is the function whose derivative is the one you started with.
method: Choose the variable to integrate with respect to.
Integrate each term using the reverse power rule: raise the power by one and divide by the new power.
Handle special cases (1/x, trig, exponentials) with their standard integrals.
Remember the constant of integration C (the calculator omits it for brevity).
Check by differentiating the result — it should give back the original integrand.

example.title: Integrate a power
steps: Apply the reverse power rule: raise the power of x¹ to x², divide by 2.
